content: I'll start by reading the required context files to understand my task.

1	# Archon Project
2	
3	You are one of: the plan agent, a prover agent, a subagent (per descriptor in `.archon/subagents/`), or the review agent. Read `PROGRESS.md` to determine your role and current objectives. Keep workspace tidy. Prefer existing MCP tools.
4	
5	## Priority Rule
6	
7	When instructions conflict between global and local sources, **local takes precedence**:
8	
9	- Prompts in `.archon/prompts/` override Archon's global prompts.
10	- Skills in `.claude/skills/` override globally installed plugins.
11	- Rules in `.claude/rules/` apply only to this project.
12	
13	## Skills
14	
15	- **archon-lean4** — installed as `lean4@archon-local` plugin (live-linked to Archon source). Provides `/archon-lean4:prove`, `/archon-lean4:golf`, `/archon-lean4:doctor`, etc.
16	
17	## Tools
18	
19	Project tools live in `.claude/tools/` as directly-executable scripts. List with `ls .claude/tools/`; run `<path> --help`. MCP servers (Lean LSP, etc.) are registered in `.claude/settings.json` / `.mcp.json` and surface as `mcp__*` tools.
20	
21	Two always-present scripts:
22	
23	- **`archon-informal-agent.py`** — call external LLMs for an informal proof-style sketch when you're stuck on an approach and want a second opinion. Supports DeepSeek, Kimi (OpenAI-compatible via `--provider kimi`), Kimi (Anthropic-compatible via `--provider kimi-anthropic`), OpenRouter, OpenAI, and Gemini; defaults to `--provider auto` which picks the best available key automatically. **Requires at least one key in env** (`DEEPSEEK_API_KEY` / `MOONSHOT_API_KEY` / `OPENROUTER_API_KEY` / `OPENAI_API_KEY` / `GEMINI_API_KEY`) — check via `env | grep -E "DEEPSEEK|MOONSHOT|OPENROUTER|OPENAI|GEMINI"` BEFORE planning around its use; if no key is set, fall back to alternatives below. Both `kimi` and `kimi-anthropic` use the same `MOONSHOT_API_KEY`; the latter speaks Moonshot's Anthropic-compatible Messages endpoint and supports `--think`. The output is LLM-generated from training data, NOT source-derived: this tool is **not** a literature retriever. For actual literature lookup (cross-check a claim against Hartshorne, fetch an arXiv paper, summarize a Stacks tag) consult the auto-injected subagent catalog — when a literature/reference-fetching subagent is enabled it will appear there — or use the `WebSearch` / `WebFetch` tools directly.
24	- **`archon-subagent.py`** — the generic subagent dispatcher (one wrapper handles every subagent — see "Subagents" below).
25	
26	## Subagents
27	
28	Subagents are descriptor files at `.archon/subagents/<name>.md` (YAML frontmatter — `name`, `description`, `write_domain`, `read_only`, `can_spawn`, `default_enabled`, optional `mandatory: [<phase>...]`, optional `dispatcher_notes` — followed by the prompt body the spawned Claude reads).
29	
30	**You do NOT need to discover subagents.** The plan and review prompts auto-inject an **Available subagents** section at the top of each invocation listing every enabled descriptor with description, write-domain, MANDATORY flag, and `dispatcher_notes`. When you decide to invoke a subagent, read its full prompt at `.archon/subagents/<name>.md`.
31	
32	**Invoke** via the generic wrapper (Bash tool, foreground):
33	
34	```
35	python3 .claude/tools/archon-subagent.py \
36	  --name <subagent-name> \
37	  --slug <kebab-slug> \
38	  --directive-file <path-to-directive.md> \
39	  --write-domain '<glob>'        # repeat for multiple
40	```
41	
42	The wrapper exits 0 on success, prints a one-line status, writes the report to `.archon/task_results/<name>-<slug>.md`, and the Archon CLI auto-archives a copy to `logs/iter-NNN/<name>-<slug>-report.md` for the dashboard. The dispatch semaphore caps concurrent subagent processes at `loop.max_parallel`; the wrapper resolves `--parent-slug` from `ARCHON_SUBAGENT_SLUG` so nested children inherit the hierarchy.
43	
44	**Treat each dispatch as blocking — wait for its report before acting on it.** Don't *deliberately* set `run_in_background: true`. But note the wrapper is genuinely synchronous (it `subprocess.run`s the `archon subagent` CLI and only returns once the child finishes and its report is on disk), so the harness may *auto-background* a long-running dispatch and hand you a task ID immediately — that's expected and fine. When it does, await that task / poll for the report file at `.archon/task_results/<name>-<slug>.md` (the wrapper's one-line status and exit 0 confirm completion) before you read or act on the result. The thing to avoid is firing a dispatch and then continuing to plan as if it had already returned; whether the call blocked inline or was auto-backgrounded, the rule is the same: do not consume a subagent's output until its report exists.
45	
46	**Highly-recommended subagents.** A descriptor whose frontmatter sets `mandatory: [plan]` is treated as **highly recommended** for the plan phase (similarly `[review]` for the review phase). The catalog tags them `[HIGHLY RECOMMENDED]`. Dispatch each one unless you have a concrete reason to skip — when skipping, record a one-line rationale under a `## Subagent skips` section in `iter/iter-NNN/<phase>.md` (e.g. `- strategy-critic: STRATEGY.md SHA unchanged from prior iter and last verdict was SOUND`). A post-phase audit checks both signals: it silences when the subagent dispatched OR a skip rationale was recorded, and warns only when neither happened. Each subagent's `dispatcher_notes` enumerates its specific skip conditions — read them before deciding. **Subagents ship disabled by default** (`default_enabled: false`), so the rule only fires for subagents the user has explicitly listed under `subagents.enabled` in `.archon/config.json`; on a fresh project the catalog is empty and no recommended dispatch is required. (The frontmatter field is still named `mandatory: [...]` for backward compat with existing configs and descriptors — its semantic meaning is "highly recommended", not "must dispatch".)
47	
48	## Key Files & Permissions
49	
50	All Archon state files are in `.archon/`. The table below covers **phase agents** (plan, prover, review) and the **user**. Subagent permissions are NOT a fixed row — they are descriptor-driven (`write_domain` glob in the subagent's frontmatter, enforced at dispatch time). See "Subagent permissions" after the table.
51	
52	| File | Plan | Prover | Review | User |
53	|------|------|--------|--------|------|
54	| `PROGRESS.md` | read + write | read only | read only | read |
55	| `STRATEGY.md` | read + write | — | — | read |
56	| `USER_HINTS.md` | **loop-managed** (captured + cleared by the CLI; injected into the plan prompt) | — | — | write |
57	| `task_pending.md` | read + write | read only | read only | read |
58	| `task_done.md` | read + write | read only | read only | read |
59	| `task_results/<file>.md` | read (collect) | write (own file) | read only | read |
60	| `proof-journal/` | read | — | **write** | read |
61	| `PROJECT_STATUS.md` | read | — | **write** (Knowledge Base only — session log lives in `iter/iter-NNN/review.md`) | read |
62	| `iter/iter-NNN/plan.md` | **write** (this iter only) | — | read (recent window injected) | read |
63	| `iter/iter-NNN/review.md` | read (recent window injected) | — | **write** (this iter only) | read |
64	| `iter/iter-NNN/objectives.md` | optional write | — | read | read |
65	| `archon-protected.yaml` | read | read | read | **write** |
66	| `.lean` files | — | write (own file; frozen protected signatures) | — | write (via comments) |
67	| `blueprint/src/chapters/*.tex` | **write** (informal prose, `\lean{...}` hints) | read | **write** (semantic markers: `\mathlibok`, `% NOTE:`, `\lean{...}` corrections — NOT `\leanok`) | read |
68	| `analogies/<slug>.md` | read (when relevant) | — | — | read |
69	| `TO_USER.md` | **write** (shared notice board) | **write** (shared notice board) | **write** (shared notice board) | read |
70	
71	### Subagent permissions
72	
73	Each subagent ships YAML frontmatter:
74	
75	- `write_domain: "<glob>"` — file pattern the subagent may write. Empty for purely read-only subagents (the dispatching call typically passes `--write-domain 'task_results/**'` so the subagent can still write its own report).
76	- `read_only: true | false` — when `true`, the subagent may only write its `task_results/<name>-<slug>.md`.
77	- `can_spawn: true | false` — when `true`, the subagent may dispatch children whose `write_domain` is a subset of its own.
78	
79	The dispatcher enforces `write_domain` at the filesystem level: any child whose declared domain is not a subset of the parent's is rejected before the child agent starts. Reads are restricted only by the descriptor's prompt body (e.g. fresh-context critics that are told not to read PROGRESS.md / STRATEGY.md).
80	
81	**`.archon/REFACTOR_DIRECTIVE.md`** is reserved for the interactive `archon refactor draft` / `archon refactor run` flow run by hand by the mathematician. The autonomous loop never reads or writes that file. To invoke a refactor-style subagent inside the loop, write the directive to a fresh tempfile under `.archon/logs/iter-NNN/<name>-<slug>-directive.md` and dispatch via the generic wrapper. Older state files (`STRATEGY.md`, `task_pending.md`, `PROGRESS.md`) may contain leftover references to the old REFACTOR_DIRECTIVE.md flow — treat those as historical noise.
82	
83	## Protected Declarations
84	
85	`archon-protected.yaml` at the project root lists declarations whose **signatures are frozen** by the mathematician. Rules:
86	
87	- **Plan / prover / review** agents: read-only on protected signatures. May fill proof bodies; may not rename, re-type, or reorder arguments.
88	- **Write-capable structural subagents** (per their `write_domain`): may *move* a protected declaration to a different file (name + signature preserved verbatim) and must then update the path key in `archon-protected.yaml`. They may NEVER rename / re-type / delete / re-sign a protected declaration.
89	- The YAML file itself is user-only. No agent adds or removes a declaration. The only allowed agent edit is updating an existing declaration's file path.
90	
91	## Blueprint Marker Vocabulary
92	
93	Two semantic markers:
94	
95	- `\leanok` — statement block: declaration is formalized (at least a `sorry` present). Proof block: proof closed, no `sorry`.
96	- `\mathlibok` — statement block: declaration is backed by Mathlib (re-export / alias); no Archon proof obligation remains.
97	
98	No marker means the block is unformalized. If a block fails to translate, leave it unmarked and annotate with a `% NOTE:` comment.
99	
100	**`\leanok` is managed deterministically by the `sync_leanok` phase** that runs between prover and review each iteration. It walks every chapter, looks up each `\lean{...}` declaration, runs `sorry_analyzer` + `lake env lean`, and adds/removes `\leanok` accordingly. Agents must NOT add or remove `\leanok` themselves.
101	
102	`\mathlibok`, `\lean{...}` corrections, and `% NOTE:` annotations are the review agent's domain (they require semantic judgement). The plan agent writes informal prose and `\lean{...}` hints; provers never touch the blueprint.
103	
104	## User Interaction
105	
106	The user provides hints in two places:
107	
108	- **Strategic hints** → `.archon/USER_HINTS.md`. The Archon loop captures this file's content before each plan phase, injects it into the plan agent's prompt, and resets the file to its bundled template (an HTML-comment format guide + zero bullets) after the plan phase succeeds. The HTML-comment preamble is stripped before injection so a template-only file renders as "no hints" to the planner. The plan agent does NOT read or clear that file itself. Provers never see these hints.
109	- **File-specific hints** → `/- USER: ... -/` comments directly in `.lean` files. The prover that owns that file sees them naturally.
110	
111	Archon talks back to the user through **`TO_USER.md`** — a single, *persistent* notice board surfaced as a UI banner. It is **shared and writable by every agent** (plan, prover, review). It is no longer cleared at the start of each review phase; content survives across iters until an agent prunes it. The discipline every writer follows:
112	
113	- **Concise**: the whole file stays ≤ 2–3 short bullets. It is a banner, not a log.
114	- **Relevant now**: before adding anything, read the current content and **delete any bullet that is no longer true at this point** (the blocker was resolved, the decision was overtaken, the credential got set). Never let it accumulate stale notices.
115	- **Notice board, never a question queue**: the loop is autonomous and may run unattended for many iters. Never write "awaiting your decision", an options menu, a "where to reply", or "default to X if no reply" — anything implying the loop is paused for a human. State decisions as *made* and blockers as *worked-around-as-far-as-possible*; the user steers asynchronously via `USER_HINTS.md`.
116	
117	## Agent Roles
118	
119	### Plan Agent
120	
121	- Read `.archon/prompts/plan.md` for full instructions.
122	- User hints, blueprint-doctor findings, recent iter sidecars, the subagent catalog, and the references summary are all pre-injected into your invocation prompt. You do not "go read" those files.
123	- Read `task_results/` to collect prover and subagent results, then update `task_pending.md` / `task_done.md`.
124	- Optionally invoke subagents (via the auto-injected catalog) before writing prover objectives. Subagent reports are auto-archived to `logs/iter-NNN/` by the Archon CLI.
125	- Write `PROGRESS.md` with objectives for the next prover round.
126	- Write informal prose in `blueprint/src/chapters/*.tex`. Do NOT touch markers — `\leanok` is owned by the deterministic sync; `\mathlibok` / `% NOTE:` by the review agent.
127	- Do NOT write proofs, edit `.lean` files, or fill sorries yourself.
128	
129	### Prover Agent
130	
131	- Read `PROGRESS.md` for your objectives (read only).
132	- Your **active mode** is injected directly into your invocation prompt — read it carefully, it defines your goal, workflow, and constraints for this session.
133	- When your mode specifies `read_blueprint: true`, you are permitted to **read** your assigned blueprint chapter (`blueprint/src/chapters/<your_slug>.tex`) for the informal proof sketch. You may never write to blueprint chapters.
134	- Write results to `task_results/<your_file>.md`.
135	- Write only to the `.lean` file(s) you are assigned — never edit another agent's file.
136	- Check for `/- USER: ... -/` comments in your file for file-specific hints.
137	- Do NOT edit blueprint chapters. Flag in your task result which declarations are ready for which marker; the review agent applies them.
138	
139	### Subagents
140	
141	Subagents are descriptor-driven and discovered at runtime. The catalog is auto-injected into the plan and review prompts each phase (see "Subagents" above for the wrapper invocation). Each subagent reads only what its directive points at — never `PROGRESS.md` / `STRATEGY.md` / phase-agent state — and writes a self-contained report at `task_results/<name>-<slug>.md`. Descriptors with `can_spawn: true` may dispatch children (subject to the global `loop.max_parallel` cap).
142	
143	### Review Agent
144	
145	- Read `.archon/prompts/review.md` for full instructions.
146	- Read `proof-journal/current_session/attempts_raw.jsonl` for structured prover attempt data.
147	- Write the session journal to `proof-journal/sessions/session_N/` (`summary.md`, `milestones.jsonl`, `recommendations.md`).
148	- Update `PROJECT_STATUS.md` (Knowledge Base only — session narrative goes to `iter/iter-NNN/review.md`).
149	- Maintain the semantic blueprint markers — `\mathlibok`, `\lean{...}` corrections, `% NOTE:` annotations, stale `\notready` removal. Do NOT touch `\leanok`.
150	- Do NOT write proofs, edit `.lean` files, or modify `PROGRESS.md`.
151	
152	### Loop infrastructure (no agent role)
153	
154	These run automatically between phases:
155	
156	- **Iter sidecar init** — creates `.archon/iter/iter-NNN/` at iter start so plan + review have a stable destination for per-iter narrative. Top-level files (STRATEGY.md, PROJECT_STATUS.md, task_*.md) stay bounded.
157	- **`sync_leanok`** — runs between prover and review, deterministically updating `\leanok` markers based on actual sorry counts and compilation status.
158	- **`blueprint-doctor`** — runs between prover and review, deterministically detecting orphan chapters, broken `\ref` / `\uses` / `\proves`, and new `axiom` declarations. Findings are written to `logs/iter-NNN/blueprint-doctor.{md,json}` and inlined into the next plan agent's prompt.
159

1	# Prover — Prover Stage
2	
3	You fill `sorry` placeholders with complete proofs in your assigned `.lean` file.
4	
5	## Workflow
6	
7	1. Read `PROGRESS.md` for your objectives (read only).
8	2. Read `task_pending.md` for prior attempts, dead ends, relevant lemmas on your file.
9	3. Check the `.lean` file for `/- USER: ... -/` comments (file-specific user hints).
10	4. **Read the relevant blueprint chapter before writing Lean code.** The chapter holds the mathematical proof sketch you must align with. When stuck, re-reading the blueprint is often the fastest path forward.
11	5. Replace `sorry` with Lean proofs. Push as far as possible.
12	6. **Always save partial progress in the code.** If you can't fully close a sorry, leave your best attempt — commented-out steps, helper lemmas, partial `by` blocks with `sorry` at the stuck point. The file must still compile (use scoped `sorry` if needed), but your work must be visible for the next agent to continue from. NEVER revert to a bare `sorry` — that wastes the work.
13	7. Write your results to `task_results/<your_file>.md`.
14	
15	**Write permissions**: only your assigned `.lean` file(s) and `task_results/<your_file>.md`. Do NOT edit `PROGRESS.md`, `task_pending.md`, `task_done.md`, blueprint chapters, or other agents' files.
16	
17	## Know your target in the dependency graph (leandag)
18	
19	The blueprint dependency graph is queryable **read-only** — `archon` is on PATH, `--json` is parseable (the banner goes to stderr). Use it to ground your work instead of guessing:
20	
21	- `archon dag-query node --node <label> --json` — your target's blueprint entry, its `\lean{}` pin, and status. **If the `\lean{}` pin is a `…TODO.…` placeholder, the Lean declaration does not exist yet** — you must *create* it (the typed `sorry`, or the proof if your mode builds it), not "fill" a name that isn't in the environment.
22	- `archon dag-query ancestors --node <label> --json` — your target's full dependency closure. Build on deps that are actually done (`\leanok`); a dependency still marked ∞ / `sorry` is **not** a sound foundation — cite it only if it genuinely typechecks today.
23	- `archon dag-query gaps --json` — the ∞ holes (statements with no informal proof). If your target's cone hits one, the blueprint sketch is incomplete: flag it in your task_result rather than inventing a proof to paper over it.
24	- `archon dag-query unmatched --json` — Lean decls with no blueprint entry. After you add helpers, your new names surface here until they're blueprinted — that is the debt you must report (see Logging → "Needs blueprint entry").
25	
26	The label↔Lean mapping is the `\lean{}` annotation in the blueprint block; `leandag stats` / `leandag focus` also give a project-wide view. You never *write* the graph — that's the dag/plan/review agents' job — but reading it keeps you from proving against a hollow or unfinished foundation.
27	
28	## Protected declarations
29	
30	`archon-protected.yaml` lists declarations with **frozen signatures**. You may fill their proof bodies but must not rename, re-type, reorder arguments, or weaken hypotheses. Only the mathematician edits protected signatures.
31	
32	## Talking to the user (TO_USER.md)
33	
34	`.archon/TO_USER.md` is a *persistent* shared notice board surfaced to the user as a banner. You may add a bullet **only** for something the user genuinely must act on that you hit while proving — e.g. a missing credential/dependency a sorry is gated on, or a `/- USER: -/` question you cannot resolve. Discipline:
35	
36	- **Concise + relevant**: keep the whole file ≤ 2–3 short bullets; before adding, read it and delete any bullet no longer true. Never a question queue — the loop never waits.
37	- **Concurrency**: provers run in parallel (one per file). To avoid clobbering a sibling lane's bullet, append your single bullet prefixed with your file (`- [Foo/Bar.lean] …`) rather than rewriting the whole file, and only when it's truly user-actionable. Routine "couldn't close this sorry" notes go in `task_results/<your_file>.md`, NOT here.
38	
39	## Avoid early termination
40	
41	- Don't abandon a proof prematurely. Many complex proofs run to thousands of lines.
42	- Difficulty is NOT a valid reason to leave a `sorry`.
43	- Don't delegate to "the next iteration" or "another prover" if more effort could close it.
44	- Only modify the proof for your assigned task — leave unrelated proofs untouched.
45	- **Decompose**: break the proof into smaller sub-problems (following the blueprint's lemma structure when available: L1, L2, L3, …) and solve each individually until the entire goal closes.
46	
47	## Task completion criteria
48	
49	Your task is complete ONLY when ALL of:
50	
51	1. Every `sorry` in scope is replaced with a complete proof.
52	2. Zero axioms introduced.
53	3. The file compiles cleanly.
54	
55	## Never weaken the type to dodge the proof
56	
57	When the substantive type is unattainable this iter, you have one honest move: leave `sorry` with the **intended type signature** so the type itself encodes the claim. You must NOT substitute a tautologically-true type to trivialize the body. Three patterns are banned:
58	
59	- **Reflexive-iso placeholder.** Replacing a non-trivial `X ≅ Y` (or `Nonempty (X ≅ Y)`) target with `Nonempty (X ≅ X) := ⟨Iso.refl _⟩`. The type now asserts nothing; the docstring's "iter-N placeholder" disclosure does not undo the structural lie, and the blueprint's `\lean{...}` cross-reference will resolve to this hollow declaration.
60	- **`Classical.choice` body around an explicit witness.** Writing `Classical.choice (α := X) ⟨witness⟩` to dissolve `Type` vs `Prop` friction — the witness is unreachable through any unfold, so the def is indistinguishable from `Classical.choice ⟨any-other-X⟩` downstream. Either use `Classical.choose` on the existential chain (preserves the spec), or `Nonempty.some`-with-witness; or ship a typed `sorry`.
61	- **Empty-content `proof_wanted`.** `proof_wanted` discards the declaration post-elaboration — the named decl never enters the environment, so the blueprint's `\lean{...}` cross-reference breaks. Use `theorem name : T := by sorry` with the intended signature instead.
62	
63	**Litmus test**: if you `unfold` your declaration, does it expose the named substantive content (Kähler differential module, the explicit iso, …) or does it stop at `Classical.choice` / `Iso.refl _` / nothing? If the latter, the body is structurally vacuous — ship the typed `sorry` instead.
64	
65	## When infrastructure is missing
66	
67	Do NOT report "Mathlib lacks X" and stop. Before giving up:
68	
69	1. **Use the informal agent** (`.claude/tools/archon-informal-agent.py`): "Prove [goal] without using [missing infrastructure], only Mathlib." Even an imperfect sketch is valuable.
70	2. **Try the alternative** — formalize whatever the informal agent suggests.
71	3. **If you still can't**: write the alternative sketch to `informal/<theorem_name>.md` and record in your task result what you tried, why it failed, AND the alternative route you found. "I couldn't prove X, but here's approach Y that might work because Z" is far more useful than "infrastructure missing".
72	
73	When stuck more generally: break into smaller subgoals, search Mathlib more thoroughly, prove missing helpers yourself, try alternative strategies, re-read the blueprint, use Web Search for published proofs.
74	
75	**Impossibility vs difficulty**: technical difficulty → keep trying. Mathematical impossibility → immediately backtrack and document why.
76	
77	## Proof style
78	
79	- **Never modify working proofs** — if a declaration has no `sorry` and compiles, do not touch its body.
80	- Keep edits minimal; don't delete comments or change labels; don't add unrelated declarations.
81	- Helper lemmas you introduced may be modified if they turn out wrong.
82	- Add a concise comment above each helper lemma so reuse is easy.
83	- **List every new declaration you introduce under a `## Needs blueprint entry` heading in your task_result** (name, file, and the facts its proof relies on) — see Logging below. The project keeps a 1-to-1 Lean ↔ blueprint correspondence: the review agent and planner consume this list (and `archon dag-query unmatched`) to give each new declaration a blueprint block. An unreported helper is an invisible, isolated dependency that silently corrupts the frontier — flagging it is mandatory, not optional. (You never write the blueprint yourself; you only name what needs one.)
84	- **`change` vs `show`** — `change` reshapes the goal up to defeq; `show` is purely display-level annotation. Using `show` where `change` is needed produces a linter warning. Default to `change` when in doubt.
85

94	## LSP MCP tools
95	
96	The `archon-lean-lsp` server exposes Lean LSP operations as **MCP tool calls** (full names like `mcp__archon-lean-lsp__lean_goal` / `mcp__archon-lean-lsp__lean_diagnostic_messages`). The lean4 skill reference uses short names (`lean_goal`, `lean_local_search`, …) — same tools, NOT shell binaries.
97	
98	- Always invoke through your tool-call interface, the same way as `Read` or `Grep`.
99	- **Never** call `Bash` with `lean_goal …` etc. — there is no such shell command.
100	- First LSP action: `mcp__archon-lean-lsp__lean_diagnostic_messages` on your file. If it returns `success: false` the server is cold; retry once or run `lake build` once via Bash, then retry.
101	
102	## Search protocol
103	
104	Follow `references/lean-lsp-tools-api.md`. Key priority:
105	
106	1. `lean_local_search` first.
107	2. `lean_leansearch` for semantic search — describe the mathematical content, not just the name.
108	3. `lean_loogle` for simple type patterns only.
109	4. Never use shell `find` / `grep` to locate Mathlib theorems.
110	
111	## Tooling traps
112	
113	- **Trust `goals_after`, not just empty diagnostics**: in `lean_multi_attempt`, `diagnostics: []` can be misleading (line-scoping quirks attach errors to the enclosing `by` block). Check that `goals_after` is empty or has advanced.
114	- **Beware `lean_run_code` with imports**: standalone snippets can silently swallow elaboration errors when they contain imports. Rely on the actual file's `lean_diagnostic_messages` for authoritative verification.
115

Log negative search results ("Searched 'projective module infinite rank' — nothing in Mathlib"). The plan agent merges these into `task_pending.md` / `task_done.md` next iter. **Always include the `## Needs blueprint entry` section when you added any new declaration** (omit it only if you added none) — it is how the loop keeps Lean ↔ blueprint at 1-to-1.

8	/-!
9	# Generic flatness of a coherent sheaf (A.2.a)
10	
11	This file packages the classical Grothendieck/Nitsure~\S4 **generic flatness**
12	theorem: over a noetherian integral base `S`, a coherent `𝓞_X`-module on a
13	finite-type morphism `X ⟶ S` becomes flat after restricting to a non-empty open
14	subscheme of `S`. This is the inductive engine underlying the
15	flattening-stratification construction of Grothendieck's existence theorem for
16	the Quot scheme.
17	
18	Each blueprint-pinned declaration carries the *intended* substantive type
19	signature (matching the `\lean{...}` pin in
20	`blueprint/src/chapters/Picard_FlatteningStratification.tex`) with a `sorry`
21	body where the proof is not yet supplied. The substantive generic-flatness
22	proof is deep (Nitsure~\S4: prime-filtration dévissage + Noether normalisation +
23	clearing denominators).
24	
25	The blueprint-pinned declaration is:
26	
27	- `AlgebraicGeometry.genericFlatness` (theorem) — **generic flatness**
28	  [Nitsure §4 Theorem]: over a noetherian integral base `S`, a coherent sheaf
29	  on a finite-type `X ⟶ S` is flat over a non-empty open `V ⊆ S`. Its
30	  algebraic form (`thm:generic_flatness_algebraic`, no Lean pin) is the
31	  statement that a finite module over a finite-type algebra over a noetherian
32	  domain becomes free after inverting one non-zero element of the base.
33	
34	The file-internal `GenericFreeness` namespace lands the **finite-module /
35	finite-morphism special case** of the algebraic form axiom-clean, as a reusable
36	building block for the dévissage's leaves.
37	
38	## Note on type expressivity
39	
40	Following the project rule "Never weaken the type to dodge the proof",
41	`genericFlatness` requires the existence of a *non-empty* open `V` and
42	substantive flatness on every affine `U ⊆ V`; both quantifiers are necessary
43	(without them the statement collapses to `V = ∅`).
44	
45	## Mathlib status
46	
47	Mathlib (master `b80f227`) provides `Module.Flat`, `AlgebraicGeometry.Flat`
48	(morphism-level), `IsImmersion`, `IsLocallyNoetherian`, `IsIntegral`,
49	`LocallyOfFiniteType`, and the dévissage / Noether-normalisation infrastructure
50	the algebraic generic-flatness argument consumes. It does NOT yet provide the
51	polynomial-ring core of generic freeness, nor the geometric `genericFlatness`
52	statement packaging it over a noetherian integral base.
53	
54	## References
55	
56	Blueprint: `blueprint/src/chapters/Picard_FlatteningStratification.tex`.
57	Source: Nitsure, "Construction of Hilbert and Quot schemes", §4 (FGA Explained
58	Ch. 5, arXiv:math/0504020 pp. 5–18); Stacks Project tag 00HB (module flat).
59	-/

67	namespace AlgebraicGeometry
68	
69	/-! ## Project-local Mathlib supplement — algebraic generic freeness (finite case)
70	
71	This section builds the **module-theoretic** generic-freeness statements that
72	underlie the geometric `genericFlatness` (blueprint
73	`thm:generic_flatness_algebraic`, Nitsure~\S4 "Lemma on Generic Flatness").
74	
75	The full algebraic statement — `A` a noetherian domain, `B` a *finite-type*
76	`A`-algebra, `M` a finite `B`-module ⟹ `∃ f ≠ 0` with `M_f` free over `A_f` —
77	is a deep theorem (prime-filtration dévissage + Noether normalisation +
78	clearing denominators). Mathlib already supplies most of the dévissage
79	machinery (`IsNoetherianRing.induction_on_isQuotientEquivQuotientPrime`,
80	`ModuleCat.free_shortExact`, `exists_finite_inj_algHom_of_fg`), but it does
81	**not** yet contain the polynomial-ring core (generic freeness for a finite
82	module over `A[X₁,…,X_d]`). See the file `task_results` handoff for the
83	precise decomposition of the remaining gap.
84	
85	What we *can* land axiom-clean here is the **finite-module / finite-morphism
86	case**: when `M` is finite as an `A`-module (in particular when `B` is
87	module-finite over `A`), generic freeness follows directly from
88	`Module.FinitePresentation.exists_free_localizedModule_powers` applied at the
89	generic point `Frac A`, where `M ⊗_A Frac A` is a finite vector space hence
90	free. This is a genuine special case of the algebraic generic-freeness
91	theorem (the case of a *finite* morphism `X → S`), and a reusable building
92	block for the dévissage's leaves. -/
93	
94	namespace GenericFreeness
95	
96	/-- **Generic freeness, finite-module case.** For a noetherian integral domain
97	`A` and a finite `A`-module `M`, there is a non-zero `f ∈ A` such that the
98	localisation `M_f` is free over `A_f = Localization.Away f`.
99	
100	This is the `d = 0` (finite-morphism) special case of the algebraic
101	generic-flatness theorem (`thm:generic_flatness_algebraic`, Nitsure~\S4):
102	inverting the generic point `Frac A`, the localised module is a finite vector
103	space hence free, and `Module.FinitePresentation.exists_free_localizedModule_powers`
104	descends that freeness to a single basic open `D(f) ⊆ Spec A`. -/
105	theorem exists_free_localizationAway_of_finite
106	    (A : Type*) (M : Type*) [CommRing A] [IsDomain A] [IsNoetherianRing A]
107	    [AddCommGroup M] [Module A M] [Module.Finite A M] :
108	    ∃ f : A, f ≠ 0 ∧
109	      Module.Free (Localization.Away f) (LocalizedModule (Submonoid.powers f) M) := by
110	  haveI : Module.FinitePresentation A M := Module.finitePresentation_of_finite A M
111	  obtain ⟨r, hr, hfree, _⟩ :=
112	    Module.FinitePresentation.exists_free_localizedModule_powers (nonZeroDivisors A)
113	      (LocalizedModule.mkLinearMap (nonZeroDivisors A) M) (FractionRing A)
114	  exact ⟨r, nonZeroDivisors.ne_zero hr, hfree⟩
115	
116	/-- **Generic flatness, finite-module case.** The flatness form of
117	`exists_free_localizationAway_of_finite`: for a noetherian domain `A` and a
118	finite `A`-module `M`, there is a non-zero `f` with `M_f` flat over `A_f`.
119	This is the affine-local content of `genericFlatness` for a finite morphism. -/
120	theorem exists_flat_localizationAway_of_finite
121	    (A : Type*) (M : Type*) [CommRing A] [IsDomain A] [IsNoetherianRing A]
122	    [AddCommGroup M] [Module A M] [Module.Finite A M] :
123	    ∃ f : A, f ≠ 0 ∧
124	      Module.Flat (Localization.Away f) (LocalizedModule (Submonoid.powers f) M) := by
125	  obtain ⟨f, hf, hfree⟩ := exists_free_localizationAway_of_finite A M
126	  haveI := hfree
127	  exact ⟨f, hf, Module.Flat.of_free⟩
128	
129	/-- **Generic freeness, finite-morphism case.** If `A` is a noetherian domain,
130	`B` a *module-finite* `A`-algebra, and `M` a finite `B`-module (with the
131	compatible `A`-module structure), then there is a non-zero `f ∈ A` with `M_f`
132	free over `A_f`. Reduces to `exists_free_localizationAway_of_finite` via
133	`Module.Finite.trans` (a finite module over a module-finite algebra is finite
134	over the base). This is generic flatness for a *finite* morphism `X → S`. -/
135	theorem exists_free_localizationAway_of_moduleFinite
136	    (A : Type*) (B : Type*) (M : Type*)
137	    [CommRing A] [IsDomain A] [IsNoetherianRing A]
138	    [CommRing B] [Algebra A B] [Module.Finite A B]
139	    [AddCommGroup M] [Module B M] [Module.Finite B M]
140	    [Module A M] [IsScalarTower A B M] :
141	    ∃ f : A, f ≠ 0 ∧
142	      Module.Free (Localization.Away f) (LocalizedModule (Submonoid.powers f) M) := by
143	  haveI : Module.Finite A M := Module.Finite.trans B M
144	  exact exists_free_localizationAway_of_finite A M
145	
146	/-! ### Dévissage chain for the finite-type residue (Nitsure §4)
147	
148	The following sub-lemmas decompose the surviving residue of
149	`genericFlatnessAlgebraic` — the case where `M` is finite over the finite-type
150	`A`-algebra `B` but not module-finite over `A` — into the classical Nitsure §4
151	induction. They are ordered so each step uses only earlier blocks (or Mathlib
152	anchors). Throughout, `A` is a noetherian domain with fraction field
153	`K = Frac A`, `B` a finite-type `A`-algebra, and `M` a finite `B`-module with
154	the compatible `A`-module structure via the scalar tower `A → B → M`. -/
155	
156	/-- **L1 — torsion base case `M_K = 0`** (`lem:gf_torsion_base`, Nitsure §4 base
157	case of the induction). If the generic localisation `M_K = K ⊗_A M` (encoded as
158	`LocalizedModule (nonZeroDivisors A) M`) is trivial and `M` is a finite
159	`B`-module, then there is a non-zero `f ∈ A` with `f • M = 0`; consequently
160	`M_f = 0` is free over `A_f`.
161	
162	The hypothesis `Subsingleton (LocalizedModule (nonZeroDivisors A) M)` is the
163	formal encoding of `M_K = 0`: localising `M` at the non-zero divisors of the
164	domain `A` inverts exactly the non-zero elements, so `M_K = S^{-1}M` with
165	`S = A \ {0}`. Each generator of the finite `B`-module `M` is then killed by a
166	non-zero element of `A`; the product `f` of those annihilators (non-zero since
167	`A` is a domain) annihilates all of `M`, whence `M_f` is subsingleton and free. -/
168	theorem exists_free_localizationAway_of_torsion
169	    (A B M : Type*) [CommRing A] [IsDomain A]
170	    [CommRing B] [Algebra A B]
171	    [AddCommGroup M] [Module B M] [Module.Finite B M]
172	    [Module A M] [IsScalarTower A B M]
173	    (htors : Subsingleton (LocalizedModule (nonZeroDivisors A) M)) :
174	    ∃ f : A, f ≠ 0 ∧
175	      Module.Free (Localization.Away f) (LocalizedModule (Submonoid.powers f) M) := by
176	  -- The `A`/`B` scalar actions commute (A acts through `algebraMap A B`).
177	  have hcomm : ∀ (a : A) (b : B) (x : M), a • b • x = b • a • x := fun a b x => by
178	    rw [← IsScalarTower.algebraMap_smul B a (b • x), ← IsScalarTower.algebraMap_smul B a x,
179	      smul_smul, smul_smul, mul_comm]
180	  -- Torsion: every element of `M` is killed by a non-zero divisor of `A`.
181	  rw [LocalizedModule.subsingleton_iff] at htors
182	  choose r hr using htors
183	  -- `r m ∈ A⁰` and `(r m) • m = 0`.
184	  -- A finite `B`-generating set of `M`.
185	  obtain ⟨S, hS⟩ := (Module.Finite.fg_top : (⊤ : Submodule B M).FG)
186	  -- The product of the chosen annihilators of the generators.
187	  set f : A := ∏ x ∈ S, r x with hf_def
188	  have hfmem : f ∈ nonZeroDivisors A := prod_mem fun x _ => (hr x).1
189	  have hf0 : f ≠ 0 := nonZeroDivisors.ne_zero hfmem
190	  -- `f` annihilates every generator, hence all of `span B S = ⊤ = M`.
191	  have hgen : ∀ x ∈ S, f • x = (0 : M) := by
192	    intro x hx
193	    classical
194	    rw [hf_def, ← Finset.prod_erase_mul S r hx, mul_smul, (hr x).2, smul_zero]
195	  have hfM : ∀ m : M, f • m = (0 : M) := by
196	    intro m
197	    have hmem : m ∈ Submodule.span B (↑S : Set M) := by rw [hS]; exact Submodule.mem_top
198	    induction hmem using Submodule.span_induction with
199	    | mem x hx => exact hgen x hx
200	    | zero => exact smul_zero f
201	    | add x y _ _ ihx ihy => rw [smul_add, ihx, ihy, add_zero]
202	    | smul b x _ ih => rw [hcomm, ih, smul_zero]
203	  -- `f • M = 0` ⟹ `M_f` subsingleton ⟹ free over `A_f`.
204	  refine ⟨f, hf0, ?_⟩
205	  haveI : Subsingleton (LocalizedModule (Submonoid.powers f) M) :=
206	    LocalizedModule.subsingleton_iff.mpr fun m => ⟨f, Submonoid.mem_powers f, hfM m⟩
207	  exact Module.Free.of_subsingleton _ _
208	
209	/-- **L3a — localisation of a short exact sequence is exact**
210	(`lem:gf_splice_shortExact_localized_exact`, Nitsure §4). Given a short exact
211	sequence `0 → M' → M → M'' → 0` of `B`-modules and `f ∈ A`, restricting scalars
212	along `A → B` and localising every term at the powers of `f` yields a short
213	exact sequence `0 → M'_f → M_f → M''_f → 0` of `A_f`-modules; the localised
214	`i_f` is injective and the localised `q_f` is surjective.
215	
216	Localisation of modules at a multiplicative set is exact, so it preserves the
217	injection `i`, the surjection `q`, and exactness at the middle term
218	(`LocalizedModule.map_injective` / `…map_surjective` / `…map_exact` applied to
219	the scalar-restricted maps). -/
220	theorem exact_localizedModule_powers_of_shortExact
221	    (A B M' M M'' : Type*) [CommRing A]
222	    [CommRing B] [Algebra A B]
223	    [AddCommGroup M'] [Module B M'] [Module A M'] [IsScalarTower A B M']
224	    [AddCommGroup M] [Module B M] [Module A M] [IsScalarTower A B M]
225	    [AddCommGroup M''] [Module B M''] [Module A M''] [IsScalarTower A B M'']
226	    (i : M' →ₗ[B] M) (q : M →ₗ[B] M'')
227	    (hi : Function.Injective i) (hq : Function.Surjective q)
228	    (hexact : Function.Exact i q)
229	    (f : A) :
230	    Function.Injective (LocalizedModule.map (Submonoid.powers f) (i.restrictScalars A)) ∧
231	    Function.Surjective (LocalizedModule.map (Submonoid.powers f) (q.restrictScalars A)) ∧
232	    Function.Exact (LocalizedModule.map (Submonoid.powers f) (i.restrictScalars A))
233	      (LocalizedModule.map (Submonoid.powers f) (q.restrictScalars A)) := by
234	  refine ⟨LocalizedModule.map_injective _ (i.restrictScalars A) hi,
235	    LocalizedModule.map_surjective _ (q.restrictScalars A) hq, ?_⟩
236	  exact LocalizedModule.map_exact (Submonoid.powers f) (i.restrictScalars A)
237	    (q.restrictScalars A) hexact
238	
239	/-- **L3b — free transport across a finer localisation**
240	(`lem:gf_splice_shortExact_free_transport`, Nitsure §4). Let `N` be an
241	`A`-module and `f', f'' ∈ A` with `f = f' f''`. If `N_{f'}` is free over
242	`A_{f'}`, then `N_f` is free over `A_f`.
243	
244	Because `f = f' f''`, the localisation `A_f` is a localisation of `A_{f'}` at the
245	image of `f''` (`IsLocalization.Away.awayToAwayLeft` / `IsLocalization.Away.mul'`),
246	and `N_f` is the corresponding localisation of `N_{f'}`; a localisation of a free
247	module is free over the localised base ring (`Module.free_of_isLocalizedModule`). -/
248	theorem free_localizationAway_of_free_of_eq_mul
249	    (A N : Type*) [CommRing A] [AddCommGroup N] [Module A N]
250	    {f f' f'' : A} (hf : f = f' * f'')
251	    (hN' : Module.Free (Localization.Away f')
252	      (LocalizedModule (Submonoid.powers f') N)) :
253	    Module.Free (Localization.Away f)
254	      (LocalizedModule (Submonoid.powers f) N) := by
255	  subst hf
256	  haveI : Module.Free (Localization.Away f')
257	    (LocalizedModule (Submonoid.powers f') N) := hN'
258	  -- `A_f` also localises `f'' * f'` (= `f' * f''`).
259	  haveI hloc : IsLocalization.Away (f'' * f') (Localization.Away (f' * f'')) := by
260	    rw [mul_comm]; infer_instance
261	  -- The ring map `A_{f'} → A_{f'f''}` and the `A`-scalar-tower it sits in.
262	  letI algA' : Algebra (Localization.Away f') (Localization.Away (f' * f'')) :=
263	    (IsLocalization.Away.awayToAwayLeft (R := A) (S := Localization.Away f')
264	      (P := Localization.Away (f' * f'')) f' f'').toAlgebra
265	  haveI htower : IsScalarTower A (Localization.Away f') (Localization.Away (f' * f'')) :=
266	    IsScalarTower.of_algebraMap_eq fun a =>
267	      (IsLocalization.Away.awayToAwayLeft_eq (S := Localization.Away f') f' f'' a).symm
268	  -- Restrict scalars along `A_{f'} → A_{f'f''}` to make `N_f` an `A_{f'}`-module.
269	  letI modA' : Module (Localization.Away f') (LocalizedModule (Submonoid.powers (f' * f'')) N) :=
270	    Module.compHom _ (algebraMap (Localization.Away f') (Localization.Away (f' * f'')))
271	  haveI towerA'Af : IsScalarTower (Localization.Away f') (Localization.Away (f' * f''))
272	      (LocalizedModule (Submonoid.powers (f' * f'')) N) :=
273	    IsScalarTower.of_algebraMap_smul fun _ _ => rfl
274	  haveI towerAA' : IsScalarTower A (Localization.Away f')
275	      (LocalizedModule (Submonoid.powers (f' * f'')) N) := by
276	    refine ⟨fun a a' x => ?_⟩
277	    have hcompA' : ∀ (b : Localization.Away f')
278	        (y : LocalizedModule (Submonoid.powers (f' * f'')) N),
279	        b • y = algebraMap (Localization.Away f') (Localization.Away (f' * f'')) b • y :=
280	      fun _ _ => rfl
281	    rw [hcompA' (a • a') x, hcompA' a' x, Algebra.smul_def, map_mul,
282	      ← IsScalarTower.algebraMap_apply A (Localization.Away f') (Localization.Away (f' * f'')) a,
283	      mul_smul, IsScalarTower.algebraMap_smul]
284	  -- `f'` acts invertibly on `N_f`: `f' * f''` does, and the commuting factor `f'` inherits it.
285	  have hunit : ∀ x : Submonoid.powers f',
286	      IsUnit (algebraMap A (Module.End A (LocalizedModule (Submonoid.powers (f' * f'')) N))
287	        (x : A)) := by
288	    have hprod : IsUnit (algebraMap A
289	        (Module.End A (LocalizedModule (Submonoid.powers (f' * f'')) N)) (f' * f'')) :=
290	      IsLocalizedModule.map_units
291	        (LocalizedModule.mkLinearMap (Submonoid.powers (f' * f'')) N)
292	        ⟨f' * f'', Submonoid.mem_powers _⟩
293	    rw [map_mul] at hprod
294	    have hcomm : Commute
295	        (algebraMap A (Module.End A (LocalizedModule (Submonoid.powers (f' * f'')) N)) f')
296	        (algebraMap A (Module.End A (LocalizedModule (Submonoid.powers (f' * f'')) N)) f'') :=
297	      (Commute.all _ _).map _
298	    have hf'unit := (hcomm.isUnit_mul_iff.mp hprod).1
299	    rintro ⟨x, n, rfl⟩
300	    simpa [map_pow] using hf'unit.pow n
301	  -- The `A`-linear localisation map `N_{f'} → N_f`, upgraded to `A_{f'}`-linear.
302	  let φ : LocalizedModule (Submonoid.powers f') N →ₗ[A]
303	      LocalizedModule (Submonoid.powers (f' * f'')) N :=
304	    IsLocalizedModule.lift (Submonoid.powers f') (LocalizedModule.mkLinearMap _ N)
305	      (LocalizedModule.mkLinearMap _ N) hunit
306	  let h : LocalizedModule (Submonoid.powers f') N →ₗ[Localization.Away f']
307	      LocalizedModule (Submonoid.powers (f' * f'')) N :=
308	    LinearMap.extendScalarsOfIsLocalization (Submonoid.powers f') (Localization.Away f') φ
309	  -- `N_{f'}` is the base change of `N` to `A_{f'}`; the composite to `N_f` is the
310	  -- base change of `N` to `A_f`, so by cancellation `h` is the base change to `A_f`.
311	  have hbcA' : IsBaseChange (Localization.Away f')
312	      (LocalizedModule.mkLinearMap (Submonoid.powers f') N) :=
313	    IsLocalizedModule.isBaseChange (Submonoid.powers f') (Localization.Away f') _
314	  have hcomp : (LinearMap.restrictScalars A h) ∘ₗ
315	      (LocalizedModule.mkLinearMap (Submonoid.powers f') N)
316	      = LocalizedModule.mkLinearMap (Submonoid.powers (f' * f'')) N := by
317	    rw [LinearMap.restrictScalars_extendScalarsOfIsLocalization]
318	    exact IsLocalizedModule.lift_comp _ _ _ hunit
319	  have hbcAf : IsBaseChange (Localization.Away (f' * f''))
320	      ((LinearMap.restrictScalars A h) ∘ₗ
321	        (LocalizedModule.mkLinearMap (Submonoid.powers f') N)) := by
322	    rw [hcomp]
323	    exact IsLocalizedModule.isBaseChange (Submonoid.powers (f' * f''))
324	      (Localization.Away (f' * f'')) _
325	  exact (hbcA'.of_comp hbcAf).free
326	
327	/-- **L3c — a short exact sequence with free ends has a free middle**
328	(`lem:gf_splice_shortExact_split`, Nitsure §4). Let
329	`0 → P → Q → T → 0` be a short exact sequence of `R`-modules with both ends `P`
330	and `T` free over `R`. Then `Q` is free over `R`.
331	
332	The free quotient `T` is projective, so the surjection `Q → T` admits a section
333	(`Module.projective_lifting_property`); the splitting
334	(`Function.Exact.splitSurjectiveEquiv`) gives `Q ≃ₗ[R] P × T`, and `P × T` is
335	free, hence so is `Q` (`Module.Free.of_equiv`). -/
336	theorem free_of_shortExact_of_free_free
337	    (R P Q T : Type*) [CommRing R]
338	    [AddCommGroup P] [Module R P] [Module.Free R P]
339	    [AddCommGroup Q] [Module R Q]
340	    [AddCommGroup T] [Module R T] [Module.Free R T]
341	    (iota : P →ₗ[R] Q) (pi : Q →ₗ[R] T)
342	    (hiota : Function.Injective iota) (hpi : Function.Surjective pi)
343	    (hexact : Function.Exact iota pi) :
344	    Module.Free R Q := by
345	  obtain ⟨l, hl⟩ := Module.projective_lifting_property pi LinearMap.id hpi
346	  obtain ⟨e, -⟩ := hexact.splitSurjectiveEquiv hiota ⟨l, hl⟩
347	  exact Module.Free.of_equiv e.symm
348	
349	/-- **L3 — splicing fact for a short exact sequence** (`lem:gf_splice_shortExact`,
350	Nitsure §4). Given a short exact sequence `0 → M' → M → M'' → 0` of `B`-modules
351	and non-zero `f', f'' ∈ A` such that `M'_{f'}` is free over `A_{f'}` and
352	`M''_{f''}` is free over `A_{f''}`, then for `f := f' f''` the localisation
353	`M_f` is free over `A_f`.
354	
355	Proof: localisation at the powers of `f` is exact, so it carries the SES to a
356	SES `0 → M'_f → M_f → M''_f → 0` of `A_f`-modules. Both ends become free over
357	`A_f` (a free module over `A_{f'}` stays free under the further localisation
358	`A_{f'} → A_f`, via `Module.free_of_isLocalizedModule`), and a SES with free
359	(hence projective) quotient `M''_f` splits, so `M_f ≅ M'_f ⊕ M''_f` is free. -/
360	theorem exists_free_localizationAway_of_shortExact
361	    (A B M' M M'' : Type*) [CommRing A] [IsDomain A]
362	    [CommRing B] [Algebra A B]
363	    [AddCommGroup M'] [Module B M'] [Module A M'] [IsScalarTower A B M']
364	    [AddCommGroup M] [Module B M] [Module A M] [IsScalarTower A B M]
365	    [AddCommGroup M''] [Module B M''] [Module A M''] [IsScalarTower A B M'']
366	    (i : M' →ₗ[B] M) (q : M →ₗ[B] M'')
367	    (hi : Function.Injective i) (hq : Function.Surjective q)
368	    (hexact : Function.Exact i q)
369	    {f' f'' : A} (hf' : f' ≠ 0) (hf'' : f'' ≠ 0)
370	    (hM' : Module.Free (Localization.Away f') (LocalizedModule (Submonoid.powers f') M'))
371	    (hM'' : Module.Free (Localization.Away f'') (LocalizedModule (Submonoid.powers f'') M'')) :
372	    ∃ f : A, f ≠ 0 ∧
373	      Module.Free (Localization.Away f) (LocalizedModule (Submonoid.powers f) M) := by
374	  -- Take `f := f' * f''`, non-zero in the domain `A`.
375	  refine ⟨f' * f'', mul_ne_zero hf' hf'', ?_⟩
376	  -- (L3b) Both ends become free over `A_{f'f''}`.
377	  haveI hM'f : Module.Free (Localization.Away (f' * f''))
378	      (LocalizedModule (Submonoid.powers (f' * f'')) M') :=
379	    free_localizationAway_of_free_of_eq_mul A M' (f := f' * f'') (f' := f') (f'' := f'') rfl hM'
380	  haveI hM''f : Module.Free (Localization.Away (f' * f''))
381	      (LocalizedModule (Submonoid.powers (f' * f'')) M'') :=
382	    free_localizationAway_of_free_of_eq_mul A M'' (f := f' * f'') (f' := f'') (f'' := f')
383	      (mul_comm f' f'') hM''
384	  -- (L3a) The scalar-restricted SES stays exact after localising at the powers of `f'f''`.
385	  obtain ⟨hinj, hsurj, hex⟩ :=
386	    exact_localizedModule_powers_of_shortExact A B M' M M'' i q hi hq hexact (f' * f'')
387	  -- (L3c) A short exact sequence of `A_{f'f''}`-modules with free ends has a free middle.
388	  exact free_of_shortExact_of_free_free (Localization.Away (f' * f''))
389	    (LocalizedModule (Submonoid.powers (f' * f'')) M')
390	    (LocalizedModule (Submonoid.powers (f' * f'')) M)
391	    (LocalizedModule (Submonoid.powers (f' * f'')) M'')
392	    (LocalizedModule.map (Submonoid.powers (f' * f'')) (i.restrictScalars A))
393	    (LocalizedModule.map (Submonoid.powers (f' * f'')) (q.restrictScalars A))
394	    hinj hsurj hex
395	
396	/-- **L4a — clearing one polynomial's denominators** (`lem:gf_clear_one_denominator`,
397	Nitsure §4 common-denominator step). For `A` a noetherian domain with fraction
398	field `K = Frac A` and a polynomial `p ∈ K[X_1,…,X_n]`, there is a non-zero
399	`g ∈ A` such that `p` lies in the image of the coefficient-extension map
400	`A_g[X_1,…,X_n] → K[X_1,…,X_n]`, where `A_g = Localization.Away g`; equivalently
401	every coefficient of `p` already lies in the subring `A_g ⊆ K`.
402	
403	The denominator-clearing engine: take `g` to be a common denominator of the
404	finitely many coefficients of `p` (Mathlib's
405	`IsLocalization.exist_integer_multiples` over the support), so each coefficient
406	`c = a / g ∈ A_g`. The witness polynomial is the numerator polynomial scaled by
407	the unit `g⁻¹ ∈ A_g`. This is the shared primitive the Noether-normalisation L4
408	denominator-clear and the torsion-reindex (L5b) both consume. -/
409	theorem gf_clear_one_denominator
410	    (A : Type*) [CommRing A] [IsDomain A] (n : ℕ)
411	    (p : MvPolynomial (Fin n) (FractionRing A)) :
412	    ∃ (g : A) (hg : g ≠ 0),
413	      p ∈ Set.range (MvPolynomial.map
414	        (IsLocalization.map (FractionRing A) (RingHom.id A)
415	          (show Submonoid.powers g ≤ Submonoid.comap (RingHom.id A) (nonZeroDivisors A) by
416	            rw [Submonoid.powers_le]
417	            simpa using mem_nonZeroDivisors_of_ne_zero hg)
418	          : Localization.Away g →+* FractionRing A)) := by
419	  classical
420	  obtain ⟨s, hs⟩ := IsLocalization.exist_integer_multiples (nonZeroDivisors A)
421	    p.support (fun i => MvPolynomial.coeff i p)
422	  refine ⟨s, nonZeroDivisors.ne_zero s.2, ?_⟩
423	  have hle : Submonoid.powers (↑s : A) ≤ Submonoid.comap (RingHom.id A) (nonZeroDivisors A) := by
424	    rw [Submonoid.powers_le]; exact s.2
425	  set φ : Localization.Away (↑s : A) →+* FractionRing A :=
426	    IsLocalization.map (FractionRing A) (RingHom.id A) hle with hφ
427	  have hunit : IsUnit (algebraMap A (Localization.Away (↑s : A)) ↑s) :=
428	    IsLocalization.map_units (Localization.Away (↑s : A))
429	      (⟨↑s, Submonoid.mem_powers _⟩ : Submonoid.powers (↑s : A))
430	  have hnum : ∀ i, ∃ a : A, (algebraMap A (FractionRing A)) a =
431	      (algebraMap A (FractionRing A) ↑s) * MvPolynomial.coeff i p := by
432	    intro i
433	    by_cases hi : i ∈ p.support
434	    · obtain ⟨a, ha⟩ := hs i hi
435	      exact ⟨a, by rw [ha, Algebra.smul_def]⟩
436	    · exact ⟨0, by rw [MvPolynomial.notMem_support_iff.mp hi]; simp⟩
437	  choose a ha using hnum
438	  have hφs : φ (algebraMap A (Localization.Away (↑s:A)) ↑s)
439	      = algebraMap A (FractionRing A) ↑s := by rw [hφ, IsLocalization.map_eq]; rfl
440	  have hee : (↑(hunit.unit⁻¹) : Localization.Away (↑s:A))
441	      * algebraMap A (Localization.Away (↑s:A)) ↑s = 1 := by
442	    have h := Units.inv_mul hunit.unit
443	    rwa [hunit.unit_spec] at h
444	  have hφinv : φ (↑(hunit.unit⁻¹) : Localization.Away (↑s:A))
445	      * algebraMap A (FractionRing A) ↑s = 1 := by
446	    rw [← hφs, ← map_mul, hee, map_one]
447	  -- The preimage polynomial: the numerator polynomial scaled by the unit `g⁻¹`.
448	  refine ⟨MvPolynomial.C (↑(hunit.unit⁻¹) : Localization.Away (↑s:A))
449	        * (MvPolynomial.map (algebraMap A (Localization.Away (↑s:A)))
450	            (∑ i ∈ p.support, MvPolynomial.monomial i (a i))), ?_⟩
451	  apply MvPolynomial.ext
452	  intro i
453	  rw [MvPolynomial.coeff_map, MvPolynomial.coeff_C_mul, map_mul, MvPolynomial.coeff_map,
454	    IsLocalization.map_eq]
455	  have hcoeff : MvPolynomial.coeff i (∑ j ∈ p.support, MvPolynomial.monomial j (a j)) = a i := by
456	    rw [MvPolynomial.coeff_sum]
457	    simp only [MvPolynomial.coeff_monomial, Finset.sum_ite_eq']
458	    by_cases hi : i ∈ p.support
459	    · rw [if_pos hi]

758	Nitsure §4 inductive step). For `A` a noetherian domain and `d ≥ 0`, a finite
759	module `N` over the polynomial ring `P_d := A[X_1,…,X_d]`, there exist `m ∈ ℕ`

761	`T := N ⧸ range φ` is a torsion `P_d`-module. Equivalently, a short exact
762	sequence `0 → P_d^{⊕m} → N → T → 0` with `T` torsion.
763	
764	`m` is the generic rank: `dim_{Frac P_d} (Frac P_d ⊗_{P_d} N)`. The map `φ`
765	is the `P_d`-linear combination of `m` lifts `v : Fin m → N` whose images form a
766	`Frac P_d`-basis of the localisation `N_Q := LocalizedModule (P_d)⁰ N`. This step
767	is built over `P_d` directly — no inversion of any `g ∈ A` is required. -/
768	theorem gf_generic_rank_ses
769	    (A : Type*) [CommRing A] [IsDomain A] [IsNoetherianRing A]
770	    (d : ℕ) (N : Type*) [AddCommGroup N]
771	    [Module (MvPolynomial (Fin d) A) N] [Module.Finite (MvPolynomial (Fin d) A) N]
772	    [Module A N] [IsScalarTower A (MvPolynomial (Fin d) A) N] :
773	    ∃ (m : ℕ) (φ : (Fin m → MvPolynomial (Fin d) A)
774	        →ₗ[MvPolynomial (Fin d) A] N),
775	      Function.Injective φ ∧
776	      Module.IsTorsion (MvPolynomial (Fin d) A) (N ⧸ LinearMap.range φ) := by
777	  classical
778	  -- Notation: `P = A[X_1,…,X_d]`, its non-zero divisors `S`, fraction field `K`,
779	  -- localisation `NK = N_Q`, and the localisation map `ℓ : N → NK`.
780	  let P := MvPolynomial (Fin d) A
781	  let S := nonZeroDivisors P
782	  let K := Localization S
783	  let NK := LocalizedModule S N
784	  let ℓ : N →ₗ[P] NK := LocalizedModule.mkLinearMap S N
785	  -- `NK` is a finite-dimensional `K`-vector space; take a basis indexed by `Fin m`.
786	  let m := Module.finrank K NK
787	  let b : Module.Basis (Fin m) K NK := Module.finBasis K NK
788	  -- Lift each basis vector along `ℓ`, clearing its denominator.
789	  obtain ⟨lift, hlift⟩ :
790	      ∃ lift : Fin m → N × S, ∀ i, ((lift i).2 : P) • b i = ℓ (lift i).1 := by
791	    refine ⟨fun i => (IsLocalizedModule.surj S ℓ (b i)).choose, fun i => ?_⟩
792	    exact (IsLocalizedModule.surj S ℓ (b i)).choose_spec
793	  let v : Fin m → N := fun i => (lift i).1
794	  -- The denominators map to units of `K`.
795	  let u : Fin m → Kˣ := fun i => (IsLocalization.map_units K (lift i).2).unit
796	  have hlv : ∀ i, ℓ (v i) = (↑(u i) : K) • b i := by
797	    intro i
798	    have hu : (↑(u i) : K) = algebraMap P K ((lift i).2 : P) :=
799	      (IsLocalization.map_units K (lift i).2).unit_spec
800	    rw [hu, algebraMap_smul]
801	    exact (hlift i).symm
802	  -- The images `ℓ (v i)` are linearly independent over `K` (unit multiples of a basis).
803	  have hLIK : LinearIndependent K (fun i => ℓ (v i)) := by
804	    have h := b.linearIndependent.units_smul u
805	    have heq : (fun i => ℓ (v i)) = (u • (⇑b)) := by
806	      funext i; rw [hlv i]; rfl
807	    rw [heq]; exact h
808	  -- Restrict scalars to `P` (the inclusion `P → K` is injective), then descend to `v`.
809	  have hLIP : LinearIndependent P (fun i => ℓ (v i)) := by
810	    refine hLIK.restrict_scalars ?_
811	    intro x y hxy
812	    apply IsFractionRing.injective P K
813	    rw [Algebra.algebraMap_eq_smul_one, Algebra.algebraMap_eq_smul_one]
814	    exact hxy
815	  have hLIv : LinearIndependent P v := LinearIndependent.of_comp ℓ hLIP
816	  -- The map `φ : P^{⊕m} → N`, `e_i ↦ v i`.
817	  refine ⟨m, Fintype.linearCombination P v, ?_, ?_⟩
818	  · -- Injectivity of `φ` is exactly linear independence of `v`.
819	    rw [← LinearMap.ker_eq_bot, LinearMap.ker_eq_bot']
820	    intro g hg
821	    rw [Fintype.linearCombination_apply] at hg
822	    funext i
823	    exact Fintype.linearIndependent_iff.mp hLIv g hg i
824	  · -- The cokernel `T = N ⧸ range φ` is torsion: every element is killed by a
825	    -- non-zero divisor of `P`.
826	    intro x
827	    obtain ⟨n, rfl⟩ := Submodule.Quotient.mk_surjective _ x
828	    -- The images `ℓ (v i)` span `NK` over `K` (they are unit multiples of a basis).
829	    have hspan : Submodule.span K (Set.range (fun i => ℓ (v i))) = ⊤ := by
830	      refine top_unique ?_
831	      rw [← b.span_eq, Submodule.span_le]
832	      rintro _ ⟨i, rfl⟩
833	      have hbi : b i = (↑(u i) : K)⁻¹ • ℓ (v i) := by
834	        rw [hlv i, smul_smul, inv_mul_cancel₀ (u i).ne_zero, one_smul]
835	      rw [hbi]
836	      exact Submodule.smul_mem _ _ (Submodule.subset_span ⟨i, rfl⟩)
837	    -- Hence `ℓ n` is a `K`-linear combination of the `ℓ (v i)`.
838	    obtain ⟨c, hc⟩ := (Submodule.mem_span_range_iff_exists_fun K).mp
839	      (show ℓ n ∈ Submodule.span K (Set.range fun i => ℓ (v i)) by
840	        rw [hspan]; exact Submodule.mem_top)
841	    -- Clear the denominators of the finitely many coefficients `c i`.
842	    obtain ⟨s, hs⟩ := IsLocalization.exist_integer_multiples S Finset.univ c
843	    have ha : ∀ i, ∃ y : P, algebraMap P K y = (s : P) • c i := fun i =>
844	      hs i (Finset.mem_univ i)
845	    choose a hae using ha
846	    -- After multiplying by `s`, `ℓ (s • n)` becomes `ℓ (φ a)`.
847	    have key : ℓ ((s : P) • n) = ℓ (Fintype.linearCombination P v a) := by
848	      rw [map_smul, ← hc, Fintype.linearCombination_apply, map_sum, Finset.smul_sum]
849	      refine Finset.sum_congr rfl (fun i _ => ?_)
850	      rw [map_smul]
851	      -- `(s:P) • (c i • ℓ v_i) = a i • ℓ v_i`
852	      rw [← algebraMap_smul K ((s : P)) (c i • ℓ (v i)), smul_smul, ← Algebra.smul_def,
853	        ← hae i, algebraMap_smul]
854	    -- So `s • n - φ a` lies in the kernel of `ℓ`, hence is killed by some `t ∈ S`.
855	    have hker : ℓ ((s : P) • n - Fintype.linearCombination P v a) = 0 := by
856	      rw [map_sub, key, sub_self]
857	    obtain ⟨t, ht⟩ := (IsLocalizedModule.eq_zero_iff S ℓ).mp hker
858	    -- The non-zero divisor `t * s` annihilates the class of `n` in `T`.
859	    refine ⟨t * s, ?_⟩
860	    rw [smul_sub, sub_eq_zero] at ht
861	    have ht2 : (↑t : P) • ((↑s : P) • n) = (↑t : P) • Fintype.linearCombination P v a := ht
862	    have hmem : (↑(t * s) : P) • n ∈ LinearMap.range (Fintype.linearCombination P v) := by
863	      rw [Submonoid.coe_mul, mul_smul, ht2, ← map_smul]
864	      exact LinearMap.mem_range_self _ _
865	    -- Conclude `(t * s) • ⟦n⟧ = 0`.
866	    rw [← Submodule.Quotient.mk_smul, Submodule.Quotient.mk_eq_zero]
867	    exact hmem
868	
869	/-- **L5b.1 — annihilator extraction for the torsion module**
870	(`lem:gf_torsion_annihilator`, Nitsure §4). For `A` a noetherian domain, `d ≥ 0`,
871	and `T` a finite *torsion* module over `P_d := A[X_1,…,X_d]`, there exists a
872	non-zero `F ∈ Ann_{P_d}(T)`. Since `P_d` is a domain, a non-zero-divisor
873	annihilator (provided by `Submodule.annihilator_top_inter_nonZeroDivisors`) is the
874	same thing as a non-zero one. -/
875	theorem gf_torsion_annihilator
876	    (A : Type*) [CommRing A] [IsDomain A] [IsNoetherianRing A] (d : ℕ)
877	    (T : Type*) [AddCommGroup T] [Module (MvPolynomial (Fin d) A) T]
878	    [Module.Finite (MvPolynomial (Fin d) A) T]
879	    (htors : Module.IsTorsion (MvPolynomial (Fin d) A) T) :
880	    ∃ F : MvPolynomial (Fin d) A, F ≠ 0 ∧
881	      F ∈ Module.annihilator (MvPolynomial (Fin d) A) T := by
882	  obtain ⟨F, hFann, hFnzd⟩ := Submodule.annihilator_top_inter_nonZeroDivisors htors
883	  refine ⟨F, nonZeroDivisors.ne_zero hFnzd, ?_⟩
884	  rw [← Submodule.annihilator_top]
885	  exact hFann
886	
887	/-! ### L5b.2 — Nagata change of variables (`lem:gf_nagata_monic_lastVar`)
888	
889	The single-variable elimination of the support-dimension drop needs a triangular
890	change of variables `e` making a non-zero `F ∈ A[X_0,…,X_n]` monic in `X_0` up to a
891	unit after inverting one `g ∈ A`. Mathlib's Noether-normalisation development
892	(`Mathlib.RingTheory.NoetherNormalization`) constructs exactly this transformation
893	`T` and proves the leading coefficient is a unit — but only over a *field*, and as
894	`private` lemmas. The block below replays that construction over a noetherian *domain*
895	(the field is used in Mathlib only to conclude the top coefficient is a unit; over a
896	domain it is merely non-zero, and becomes a unit after inverting it). The degree
897	bookkeeping (`degreeOf_zero_t`, `leadingCoeff_finSuccEquiv_t`, `T_leadingcoeff_eq`) is
898	a domain-adapted transcription of the Mathlib originals. -/
899	
900	section NagataNormalization
901	
902	-- `AlgebraicGeometry.Polynomial` exists and would shadow the root namespace, so the
903	-- `Polynomial`/`MvPolynomial` lemma APIs are opened with explicit `_root_.` prefixes.
904	open _root_.Polynomial _root_.MvPolynomial _root_.Ideal Nat _root_.RingHom List
905	
906	/-- `finSuccEquiv` commutes with coefficient base change `MvPolynomial.map phi`. -/
907	private theorem finSuccEquiv_map_comm {A B : Type*} [CommRing A] [CommRing B]
908	    (phi : A →+* B) (m : ℕ) (q : MvPolynomial (Fin (m + 1)) A) :
909	    finSuccEquiv B m (MvPolynomial.map phi q)
910	      = Polynomial.map (MvPolynomial.map phi) (finSuccEquiv A m q) := by
911	  induction q using MvPolynomial.induction_on with
912	  | C a => simp [finSuccEquiv_apply]
913	  | add p q hp hq => simp [hp, hq]
914	  | mul_X p i hp =>
915	    simp only [map_mul, Polynomial.map_mul, hp]
916	    congr 1
917	    cases i using Fin.cases with
918	    | zero => simp [finSuccEquiv_X_zero]
919	    | succ j => simp [finSuccEquiv_X_succ]
920	
921	variable {k : Type*} [CommRing k] [IsDomain k] {n : ℕ} (f : MvPolynomial (Fin (n + 1)) k)
922	variable (v w : Fin (n + 1) →₀ ℕ)
923	
924	local notation3 "up" => 2 + f.totalDegree
925	local notation3 "r" => fun (i : Fin (n + 1)) ↦ up ^ i.1
926	
927	omit [IsDomain k] in
928	variable {f v} in
929	private lemma lt_up (vlt : ∀ i, v i < up) : ∀ l ∈ ofFn v, l < up := by grind
930	
931	/-- The triangular Nagata substitution `X_i ↦ X_i + c • X_0 ^ (up ^ i)` (`i ≠ 0`),
932	`X_0 ↦ X_0`. Transcribed from `Mathlib.RingTheory.NoetherNormalization` to a domain. -/
933	private noncomputable abbrev T1 (c : k) :
934	    MvPolynomial (Fin (n + 1)) k →ₐ[k] MvPolynomial (Fin (n + 1)) k :=
935	  aeval fun i ↦ if i = 0 then X 0 else X i + c • X 0 ^ r i
936	
937	omit [IsDomain k] in
938	private lemma t1_comp_t1_neg (c : k) : (T1 f c).comp (T1 f (-c)) = AlgHom.id _ _ := by
939	  rw [comp_aeval, ← MvPolynomial.aeval_X_left]; ext i v; cases i using Fin.cases <;> simp
940	
941	/-- The Nagata transformation as an `A`-algebra automorphism. -/
942	private noncomputable abbrev T := AlgEquiv.ofAlgHom (T1 f 1) (T1 f (-1))
943	  (t1_comp_t1_neg f 1) (by simpa using t1_comp_t1_neg f (-1))
944	
945	omit [IsDomain k] in
946	private lemma sum_r_mul_ne (vlt : ∀ i, v i < up) (wlt : ∀ i, w i < up) (ne : v ≠ w) :
947	    ∑ x : Fin (n + 1), r x * v x ≠ ∑ x : Fin (n + 1), r x * w x := by
948	  intro h
949	  refine ne <| Finsupp.ext <| congrFun <| ofFn_inj.mp ?_
950	  apply ofDigits_inj_of_len_eq (Nat.lt_add_right f.totalDegree one_lt_two)
951	    (by simp) (lt_up vlt) (lt_up wlt)
952	  simpa only [ofDigits_eq_sum_mapIdx, mapIdx_eq_ofFn, get_ofFn, length_ofFn,
953	    Fin.val_cast, mul_comm, sum_ofFn] using h
954	
955	private lemma degreeOf_zero_t {a : k} (ha : a ≠ 0) : ((T f) (monomial v a)).degreeOf 0 =
956	    ∑ i : Fin (n + 1), (r i) * v i := by
957	  rw [← natDegree_finSuccEquiv, monomial_eq, Finsupp.prod_pow v fun a ↦ X a]
958	  simp only [Fin.prod_univ_succ, Fin.sum_univ_succ, map_mul, map_prod, map_pow,
959	    AlgEquiv.ofAlgHom_apply, MvPolynomial.aeval_C, MvPolynomial.aeval_X, if_pos, Fin.succ_ne_zero,
960	    ite_false, one_smul, map_add, finSuccEquiv_X_zero, finSuccEquiv_X_succ, algebraMap_eq]
961	  have h (i : Fin n) :
962	      (Polynomial.C (X (R := k) i) + Polynomial.X ^ r i.succ) ^ v i.succ ≠ 0 :=
963	    pow_ne_zero (v i.succ) (leadingCoeff_ne_zero.mp <| by simp [add_comm, leadingCoeff_X_pow_add_C])
964	  rw [natDegree_mul (by simp [ha]) (mul_ne_zero (by simp) (Finset.prod_ne_zero_iff.mpr
965	    (fun i _ ↦ h i))), natDegree_mul (by simp) (Finset.prod_ne_zero_iff.mpr (fun i _ ↦ h i)),
966	    natDegree_prod _ _ (fun i _ ↦ h i), natDegree_finSuccEquiv, degreeOf_C]
967	  simpa only [natDegree_pow, zero_add, natDegree_X, mul_one, Fin.val_zero, pow_zero, one_mul,
968	    add_right_inj] using Finset.sum_congr rfl (fun i _ ↦ by
969	    rw [add_comm (Polynomial.C _), natDegree_X_pow_add_C, mul_comm])
970	
971	private lemma degreeOf_t_ne_of_ne (hv : v ∈ f.support) (hw : w ∈ f.support) (ne : v ≠ w) :
972	    (T f <| monomial v <| coeff v f).degreeOf 0 ≠
973	    (T f <| monomial w <| coeff w f).degreeOf 0 := by
974	  rw [degreeOf_zero_t _ _ <| mem_support_iff.mp hv, degreeOf_zero_t _ _ <| mem_support_iff.mp hw]
975	  refine sum_r_mul_ne f v w (fun i ↦ ?_) (fun i ↦ ?_) ne <;>
976	  exact lt_of_le_of_lt ((monomial_le_degreeOf i ‹_›).trans (degreeOf_le_totalDegree f i)) (by lia)
977	
978	private lemma leadingCoeff_finSuccEquiv_t :
979	    (finSuccEquiv k n ((T f) ((monomial v) (coeff v f)))).leadingCoeff =
980	    algebraMap k _ (coeff v f) := by
981	  rw [monomial_eq, Finsupp.prod_fintype]
982	  · simp only [map_mul, map_prod, leadingCoeff_mul, leadingCoeff_prod]
983	    rw [AlgEquiv.ofAlgHom_apply, algHom_C, algebraMap_eq, finSuccEquiv_apply, eval₂Hom_C, coe_comp]
984	    simp only [AlgEquiv.ofAlgHom_apply, Function.comp_apply, leadingCoeff_C, map_pow,
985	      leadingCoeff_pow, algebraMap_eq]
986	    have : ∀ j, ((finSuccEquiv k n) ((T1 f) 1 (X j))).leadingCoeff = 1 := fun j ↦ by
987	      by_cases h : j = 0
988	      · simp [h, finSuccEquiv_apply]
989	      · simp only [aeval_eq_bind₁, bind₁_X_right, if_neg h, one_smul, map_add, map_pow]
990	        obtain ⟨i, rfl⟩ := Fin.exists_succ_eq.mpr h
991	        simp [finSuccEquiv_X_succ, finSuccEquiv_X_zero, add_comm]
992	    simp only [this, one_pow, Finset.prod_const_one, mul_one]
993	  exact fun i ↦ pow_zero _
994	
995	/-- Over a domain, the `T`-transform of a non-zero `f` has, in `X_0`, a leading
996	coefficient equal to `C (coeff v f)` for some `v ∈ f.support` (hence non-zero). The
997	Mathlib original concludes `IsUnit` using the field hypothesis; here we keep the exact
998	coefficient so the caller can invert it. -/
999	private lemma T_leadingcoeff_eq (fne : f ≠ 0) :
1000	    ∃ v ∈ f.support,
1001	      (finSuccEquiv k n (T f f)).leadingCoeff = MvPolynomial.C (coeff v f) := by
1002	  obtain ⟨v, vin, vs⟩ := Finset.exists_max_image f.support
1003	    (fun v ↦ (T f ((monomial v) (coeff v f))).degreeOf 0) (support_nonempty.mpr fne)
1004	  set h := fun w ↦ (MvPolynomial.monomial w) (coeff w f)
1005	  simp only [← natDegree_finSuccEquiv] at vs
1006	  replace vs : ∀ x ∈ f.support \ {v}, (finSuccEquiv k n ((T f) (h x))).degree <
1007	      (finSuccEquiv k n ((T f) (h v))).degree := by
1008	    intro x hx
1009	    obtain ⟨h1, h2⟩ := Finset.mem_sdiff.mp hx
1010	    apply degree_lt_degree <| lt_of_le_of_ne (vs x h1) ?_
1011	    simpa only [natDegree_finSuccEquiv]
1012	      using degreeOf_t_ne_of_ne f _ _ h1 vin <| ne_of_not_mem_cons h2
1013	  have coeff : (finSuccEquiv k n ((T f) (h v + ∑ x ∈ f.support \ {v}, h x))).leadingCoeff =
1014	      (finSuccEquiv k n ((T f) (h v))).leadingCoeff := by
1015	    simp only [map_add, map_sum]
1016	    rw [add_comm]
1017	    apply leadingCoeff_add_of_degree_lt <| (lt_of_le_of_lt <| degree_sum_le _ _) ?_
1018	    have h2 : h v ≠ 0 := by simpa [h] using mem_support_iff.mp vin
1019	    replace h2 : (finSuccEquiv k n ((T f) (h v))) ≠ 0 := fun eq ↦ h2 <|
1020	      by simpa only [map_eq_zero_iff _ (AlgEquiv.injective _)] using eq
1021	    exact (Finset.sup_lt_iff <| Ne.bot_lt (fun x ↦ h2 <| degree_eq_bot.mp x)).mpr vs
1022	  refine ⟨v, vin, ?_⟩
1023	  rw [leadingCoeff_finSuccEquiv_t] at coeff
1024	  nth_rw 2 [← f.support_sum_monomial_coeff]
1025	  rw [Finset.sum_eq_add_sum_diff_singleton_of_mem vin h, coeff, algebraMap_eq]
